human_note: Three tightly-scoped propane support scripts for driving agent-assist knowledge cards during live or mocked rep/customer conversations. All names and account details are synthetic.

scripts.scenario_name: Missed delivery save with Spanish competitor mention
scripts.demo_purpose: Shows service recovery, bilingual trigger handling, rising churn risk, and a successful save using a replacement delivery plus a steadier billing option.
scripts.success_criterion: Customer agrees to stay after the rep books the fastest replacement delivery and offers budget billing as the approved save path.

turns.speaker: rep
turns.utterance: Thank you for calling Valley Fuel, this is Megan. Before we get started, can I verify your service address and the name on the account?

turns.speaker: customer
turns.utterance: It's Elena Morales at 18 Cedar Lane. You missed my delivery yesterday, and I took off work for that window.
expected_rep_visible_guidance: Lead with a direct apology, confirm the fastest replacement window, and review service recovery only after verifying the miss was company-caused.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.utterance: Además, me llegó un volante de NorthStar Propane con un precio mucho más bajo, así que sí estoy pensando cambiarme.
expected_rep_visible_guidance: Stay calm, show you heard the price comparison, and pivot to the best approved retention offer instead of defending margins.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.utterance: And my winter bills keep jumping around. I need something steadier if I'm going to stay.
expected_rep_visible_guidance: Acknowledge bill shock and explain budget billing as steadier monthly payments with periodic review, not a cheaper total bill.

turns.speaker: rep
turns.utterance: I'm sorry we missed that stop, Elena. I can lock in the first replacement window for tomorrow morning, and I can also quote budget billing so your monthly payments are easier to plan around.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.utterance: If the truck is coming tomorrow and you can set up the steadier payment plan, I'll stay with Valley Fuel.

scripts.scenario_name: Low-tank scare converted from will-call to auto-delivery
scripts.demo_purpose: Shows operational urgency without a full emergency, then uses next-best-action guidance to convert a repeat will-call customer to keep-full.
scripts.success_criterion: Customer accepts the earliest delivery window and agrees to switch from will-call to auto-delivery before the call ends.

turns.speaker: rep
turns.utterance: Valley Fuel support, this is Darren. What's going on today?

turns.speaker: customer
turns.utterance: My gauge is down to 15 percent, and I really do not want to run out before the weekend. How soon can someone come?
expected_rep_visible_guidance: Move straight to scheduling the earliest practical delivery and avoid scolding the customer for calling late.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.utterance: The storm pushed my last stop back, so I need to know whether Friday morning is really available or if I should expect another delay.
expected_rep_visible_guidance: Share the best known route window, avoid fake certainty, and set a callback commitment if dispatch has not locked the ETA.

turns.speaker: rep
turns.utterance: I can request the earliest Friday stop and, if dispatch changes that window, I'll make sure you get an update instead of being left guessing. Are you still on will-call?

turns.speaker: customer
turns.utterance: Yes, and honestly I'm tired of watching the gauge. What's the difference between will-call and keep-full again?
expected_rep_visible_guidance: Position auto-delivery as convenience and runout prevention, not a hard sell, and make the will-call responsibility explicit.

turns.speaker: rep
turns.utterance: With auto-delivery, we watch expected usage and schedule fills before you get this low. Since you've had another close call, switching from will-call would take the gauge-watching pressure off you.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.utterance: Go ahead and set up the earliest Friday delivery you can get and switch me to auto-delivery. I do not want another low-tank scare.

scripts.scenario_name: Contract renewal price objection with temporary hardship
scripts.demo_purpose: Shows a save path when renewal-rate friction and short-term payment stress happen in the same call, letting the rep combine retention framing with a practical payment plan.
scripts.success_criterion: Customer renews on an approved retention option and accepts a temporary payment arrangement instead of canceling service.

turns.speaker: rep
turns.utterance: Thanks for calling Valley Fuel renewals, this is Ashley. How can I help today?

turns.speaker: customer
turns.utterance: My renewal notice came in higher than last year, and if this is the new rate I may need to leave.
expected_rep_visible_guidance: Frame the renewal as an options conversation, not a defense of market pricing, and be ready to use the approved loyalty offer.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.utterance: I also cannot pay this whole balance today. Winter hit us hard, so I need a little more time and something steadier going forward.
expected_rep_visible_guidance: Stay practical and nonjudgmental: offer a payment arrangement with exact timing and explain budget billing as the future-stability option.

turns.speaker: rep
turns.utterance: We can do this in plain language. I can review the renewal options on your account, and I can split the current balance into one payment today and one on the 15th so you are not trying to solve everything at once.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.utterance: If there is a loyalty option on the renewal and I can split the balance that way, I'd rather stay than move the account.
expected_rep_visible_guidance: Use the approved loyalty or protection offer that matches the account, then close with the concrete payment-plan terms.

turns.speaker: rep
turns.utterance: Great. I'll renew you on the loyalty option available today, set the two-part payment arrangement, and send the budget-billing quote for next month so the bills are easier to plan for.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.utterance: That works for me. Renew it, split the balance the way you described, and send me the budget-billing details.
